action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 13 finally completed training (no segfault) but AP crashed due to NaN in predictions, likely because SimpleMLP forward has wrong batch handling (collapses batch dim with mean) causing identical outputs and potential numerical issues, plus possible NaN in preprocessed data from prior bad alloc. Analysis: The core issue is that SimpleMLP is not a proper graph-level MLP; it processes batched features through node_embedding then pools incorrectly. Also preprocessed .npz may be corrupted. Decision: Write a chunked, memory-safe preprocessing script (preprocess_features_v2.py) that uses memmap to build X/Y without peak memory spikes, compute stats, and save clean .npz. Write a new FeatureMLP model (model_linear.py) that directly maps fixed-size features to 128 outputs. Update train.py to use FeatureMLP with pos_weight for class imbalance, proper batch training via DataLoader, and AP evaluation. Train 15 epochs on full dataset (350k graphs) – should be fast (~seconds/epoch). Then submit eval to get first baseline AP.
